changes.result: RISK — COMMAND SYNTAX INCOMPATIBLE WITH POWERSHELL
changes.finding: The YAML validation command in the prompt uses bash heredoc syntax: 'python - <<'PY'' (line 608). This is NOT valid PowerShell and will fail as a parse error on Windows. Cursor must adapt this to PowerShell-compatible syntax. Recommended approach: python -c with the validation logic as a single-line or escaped string. Must not skip validation because the bash syntax fails — the SOP requires validation output in the final report.

CONFIRMED. estate_index_v1.yaml is the single compiled card authority. health_system_card_evidence.py hardcodes WAVE1_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS as the runtime gate. wave1_subsystem_evidence.py defers to health_system_card_evidence.py for assembly. No duplicate authority sources detected for the compiled estate.

CONFIRMED. Prohibited directories (backend/, frontend/, knowledge_bus/) are read-only in this sprint. Prohibited locked files (HEALTHIQ_AI_BETA_READINESS_DEFINITIVE_STRATEGY_FINAL_2026-06-20.md, docs/AUTHORITY_MAP.md) are read-only. Expected changed files: three docs/sprints/beta_readiness/ files only. Matrix YAML must not be placed under backend/ssot/, knowledge_bus/compiled/, or any runtime path — correctly scoped to docs/sprints/beta_readiness/.

CONFIRMED. Prompt explicitly prohibits implementing runtime system cards, promoting packages, modifying Knowledge Bus, changing scoring policy, or altering runtime behaviour.
